question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the Flux.just(current) return path in missingMessages() (when last == null || topicContext.isNext(current)) by subscribing to a topic where the first live message has a very high sequence number, causing all subsequent gap-fill queries to use incorrect boundaries and suppress gossip transactions?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the takeWhile(t -> t.getConsensusTimestamp() < filter.getEndTime()) operator by providing an endTime that is a nanosecond-precision boundary, causing a race condition where gossip transactions with consensusTimestamp == endTime are silently dropped?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: incomingMessages()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the topicListener.listen(newFilter).concatMap(t -> missingMessages(topicContext, t)) chain by subscribing to a high-frequency topic, causing concatMap to serialize all gap-fill queries and create a backlog that prevents live gossip transactions from being processed in real time?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the AtomicReference<TopicMessage> last field in TopicContext by subscribing to a topic where the live listener delivers messages concurrently, causing a lost-update race condition on last.set(topicMessage) in onNext(), leading to incorrect sequence tracking and gossip suppression?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: TopicContext.onNext()] Can an unprivileged user trigger the IllegalStateException in onNext() by subscribing to a topic where the safetyCheck and live Flux deliver the same message concurrently, causing the isNext() check to fail due to a duplicate sequence number and terminating the stream?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the Schedulers.boundedElastic() scheduler used by safetyCheck by opening many subscriptions simultaneously, exhausting the bounded elastic thread pool and causing the safety check gap recovery to queue indefinitely, preventing gossip delivery?
